roadmap.rationale: Typed Command/Notification journal split for the Workflow durable-execution primitive (GH #39; ADR-0066 / ADR-0064), closing a latent replay-corruption trap: `Started` is documented as the journal's first entry but `WorkflowEngine::start` never writes it, and the positional replay cursor cannot consume a non-`await` entry at a walked position, so a variant mismatch at the cursor silently falls through to the live path. The fix types the journal stream (D1: `JournalEntry`->`JournalCommand`{Started,RunResult,SleepArmed,SignalAwaited,ActionEmitted,Terminal} + `JournalNotification`{SignalSeen} + boundary sum `LoadedEntry`), drops the derived in-entry `step:u32` (D5), partitions at the cursor into a `Vec<JournalCommand>` positional walk + a `BTreeMap<SignalKey,JournalNotification>` correlated lookup and retires `*cursor += 2` (D2), keeps the dumb-store redb key and count-all `next_step` unchanged with the command-index derived at the cursor (D3), enforces a fail-closed determinism gate Layers 1+2 (D4), makes `Started` a real engine-written command-index-0 entry (CA-4), and extends the `replay_equivalence_provision_record` DST invariant with the Started-at-0 + notification-not-as-command cursor-advance guard (D6) on the CI critical path. Greenfield single-cut: no `#[serde(tag="v")]` envelope bump (slices 01/02/03 unlanded, no surviving on-disk journals). EXTEND-dominant per the Reuse Analysis: 8 EXTEND, 1 CREATE NEW (`LoadedEntry`, a thin boundary sum), zero new components. Deferrals carry verified GH numbers only: HA cross-node resume #205, determinism Layer 3 (content/digest) #214. Create nothing; add no new forward pointers.

phases.name: Type the journal stream: split entry, partition at the cursor, fail closed, land the regression guard
phases.description: Single-cut redesign per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` — slices 01/02/03 of the Workflow primitive are unlanded, so no on-disk journal migration and no `#[serde(tag="v")]` envelope bump (feature-delta Technology Choices). The six steps partition the surgery along verification-gate boundaries (each step lands one observable CI signal) AND along the Outside-In dependency chain: the typed enum split (01-01) is the foundation every downstream surface compiles against; the store-side `LoadedEntry` plumbing (01-02) lands the dumb-ordered-log contract; the cursor partition (01-03) is where the replay semantics actually move (`Vec<JournalCommand>` walk + `BTreeMap<SignalKey,JournalNotification>` lookup, retiring `*cursor += 2`); the fail-closed determinism gate (01-04) closes the trap's twin; the engine writing `Started` at command-index 0 (01-05) closes the trap itself; the extended `replay_equivalence_provision_record` DST invariant (01-06) is the structural regression guard that would have caught the original trap, landed last on the CI critical path. Each step is RED->GREEN->COMMIT (ADR-025), independently compiles + tests green via Lima (`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check`, `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p <crate>`), and is a single commit. RED scaffolds use `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]` per `.claude/rules/testing.md`. Mutation testing (per-feature, diff-scoped, kill-rate >= 80%) runs ONCE after 01-06, not per step.

steps.name: Split JournalEntry into JournalCommand + JournalNotification + LoadedEntry; drop in-entry step:u32
steps.description: D1 + D5. Replace the single 7-variant `JournalEntry` enum in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/journal/mod.rs` with two typed enums plus a boundary sum: `JournalCommand` { `Started{spec_digest,input_digest}`, `RunResult{name,result_digest,result_bytes}`, `SleepArmed{deadline_unix}`, `SignalAwaited{signal_key}`, `ActionEmitted{action_digest}`, `Terminal{result}` } (the replayable, cursor-advancing class) + `JournalNotification` { `SignalSeen{signal_key,value_digest,value}` } (the `SignalKey`-correlated, off-the-walk class) + `LoadedEntry = Command(JournalCommand) | Notification(JournalNotification)` (the on-disk / append / load boundary representation; the one genuinely-new type per the Reuse Analysis). DROP the in-entry `step: u32` field from RunResult/SleepArmed/SignalAwaited/ActionEmitted (and from SignalSeen) — identity is structural (position in `Vec<JournalCommand>` for commands; `SignalKey` for notifications), so a persisted `step` is a derived cache of "my own position" (`development.md` § "Persist inputs, not derived state"). All variants remain CBOR `#[serde]` (`ciborium`), no envelope bump. Re-home the 7 variants across the two leaf enums by semantic class; `SignalSeen` is the sole notification. This step lands the type shapes coherently — the control-plane crate will not fully compile against the new shapes until the call sites are updated, which is why 01-02..01-05 follow; per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'RED scaffolds', downstream call sites that do not yet compile are tracked, but this step keeps the crate's own enum module + codec module green. Update the sim proptest strategy (`journal_entry_strategy` -> a `loaded_entry_strategy` that emits `LoadedEntry::Command(...)` / `LoadedEntry::Notification(...)`, dropping the `step` generator) and the `entry_kinds` classifier helper to match on the new types. Implements D1 + D5 (CA-1, CA-2).
criteria: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/journal/mod.rs` — the single `JournalEntry` enum is replaced by `JournalCommand` (variants `Started`, `RunResult`, `SleepArmed`, `SignalAwaited`, `ActionEmitted`, `Terminal`), `JournalNotification` (sole variant `SignalSeen`), and `LoadedEntry` (`Command(JournalCommand)` | `Notification(JournalNotification)`); every type derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize`.

No `step: u32` field on any variant of `JournalCommand` or `JournalNotification` (grep `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/journal/mod.rs` for `step:` returns zero field declarations on the command/notification variants).

Each changed type's rustdoc pins pre/post/edge/invariant per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Trait definitions specify behavior' (here: type-level docs) — `JournalCommand` documents 'advances the cursor; identity is positional'; `JournalNotification` documents 'SignalKey-correlated, never advances the cursor'; `LoadedEntry` documents 'the dumb-store on-disk/append/load boundary; classes interleave in one ordered table'.

`crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/journal.rs` — the proptest strategy emits `LoadedEntry` values (a `loaded_entry_strategy` producing both `Command(...)` and `Notification(...)`), no `step` generator; the existing roundtrip property `append_then_load_round_trips_losslessly_and_in_order` is updated to assert on `Vec<LoadedEntry>` and stays GREEN.

`crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` — the `entry_kinds` classifier matches on `LoadedEntry` (mapping `Command(JournalCommand::Started)` -> "Started", ... `Notification(JournalNotification::SignalSeen)` -> "SignalSeen"); `entry_kinds_match` unchanged in shape.

`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --lib` is GREEN for the journal module + codec; downstream call sites in `workflow_runtime` / `redb` updated in 01-02..01-05 may still be RED on this step (intentional, per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits' — RED scaffolds use `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]`; call-site compile breakage is resolved by the following steps).

Commit-message shape: `feat!(workflow-journal): split JournalEntry into typed Command/Notification + LoadedEntry, drop in-entry step (D1/D5)`. Body cites ADR-0066 §2 / ADR-0064 §3 and `Step-ID: 01-01`.
steps.implementation_notes: D1: the split is a re-homing of the 7 existing variants by semantic class plus ONE genuinely-new boundary sum (`LoadedEntry`) — not new behaviour (Reuse Analysis: EXTEND (split)). `SignalSeen` is the sole `JournalNotification`; every other variant is a `JournalCommand`. `Started` and `Terminal` become first-class commands (they were second-class under the old positional walk — that is the trap). D5: dropping `step:u32` makes identity structural; per `development.md` § 'Persist inputs, not derived state' a persisted `step` is a cache of "my own position" — the store derives append-position via count-all (`next_step`, unchanged in 01-02), and the cursor derives the command-index via partition position (01-03). No `#[serde(tag="v")]` envelope bump — greenfield single-cut (Technology Choices); additive CBOR evolution still governs FUTURE await-surface variants. Keep the existing `WorkflowId` newtype + `WorkflowIdError` untouched. The proptest strategy must emit BOTH `LoadedEntry::Command(...)` and `LoadedEntry::Notification(...)` so the roundtrip property exercises the interleaved on-disk stream the store will hold; the proptest is the codec-skew backstop per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Property-based testing' (newtype/codec roundtrip is mandatory). This step's own RED bar is the updated `append_then_load_round_trips_losslessly_and_in_order` property failing against the pre-split codec, then GREEN once the split + strategy land together. Do NOT introduce `NotificationId` (D6 rejects the general correlation model — single-node Phase-1 has exactly one notification shape).
quality_gates: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --lib passes for the journal module
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim -E 'test(append_then_load_round_trips_losslessly_and_in_order)' passes
no step:u32 field on any JournalCommand/JournalNotification variant
no #[serde(tag = "v")] envelope bump
single commit
ADR-0066 + ADR-0064 cited in body
deliverables: JournalCommand enum (6 variants, no step field)

steps.name: JournalStore trait + RedbJournalStore + SimJournalStore deal in LoadedEntry; next_step count-all unchanged
steps.description: D2 (store half) + D3 (store half). Change the `JournalStore` trait surface so `append(&self, id, &LoadedEntry)` and `load_journal(&self, id) -> Vec<LoadedEntry>` deal in the boundary sum — the store stays a DUMB ORDERED LOG: it appends and loads `LoadedEntry` values in storage append-position order and never classifies (the partition into command-walk + notification-lookup is the cursor's job in 01-03, not the store's). The redb key `(WorkflowId, u32)` = storage append-position over ALL entries is UNCHANGED; `next_step` (count-all over the single table) is UNCHANGED in behaviour — only the value type the table holds changes to a CBOR-encoded `LoadedEntry`. Apply the same to `SimJournalStore`. Update the trait docstrings to pin the dumb-store contract: append-position vs command-index distinction, classes interleave in one ordered table, `next_step` counts every entry (command + notification). The Earned-Trust `probe` is unchanged except its sentinel is now a `LoadedEntry::Command(JournalCommand::Terminal{...})`. Update the store roundtrip + Earned-Trust integration tests to the `LoadedEntry` value type. Implements D2 (store) + D3 (store).
criteria: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/journal/mod.rs` — `JournalStore::append` signature is `async fn append(&self, workflow_id: &WorkflowId, entry: &LoadedEntry) -> Result<()>`; `JournalStore::load_journal` returns `Result<Vec<LoadedEntry>>`; `probe` unchanged in signature.

`JournalStore::append` / `load_journal` rustdoc pins: pre (well-formed `LoadedEntry`) / post (durable, fsync-before-return, appended at the END of the ordered run at the next storage append-position; classes interleave — a notification and a command append identically; the store does not classify) / edge (first append creates the instance's run; the store assigns the `u32` by append position over ALL entries; empty `Vec` for a fresh instance) / invariant (append order == load order; the store is a dumb ordered log — classification is the cursor's job, D2; `next_step` counts every entry).

`crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/journal/redb.rs` — value type is CBOR-encoded `LoadedEntry`; `next_step` is a count-all range scan `(id, 0)..=(id, u32::MAX)` — behaviour UNCHANGED (the count includes notifications); key `(WorkflowId, u32)` storage append-position UNCHANGED.

`crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/journal.rs` — `SimJournalStore` mirrors `RedbJournalStore`: in-memory `BTreeMap<(WorkflowId, u32), Vec<u8>>` of CBOR `LoadedEntry`; `next_step` count-all parity with the redb adapter; the probe sentinel is a `LoadedEntry`.

`crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/journal_records_inputs_not_derived.rs` — asserts an appended-then-loaded run round-trips `LoadedEntry` byte-equal, interleaving a `Command` and a `Notification`, and that `next_step` counts both; GREEN.

`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-sim --lib` is GREEN for the store layer (cursor/engine call sites updated in 01-03..01-05 may still be RED — intentional per `.claude/rules/testing.md`).
Commit-message shape: `refactor(workflow-journal): store deals in LoadedEntry; next_step count-all unchanged (D2/D3)`. Body cites ADR-0066 §3 / ADR-0064 §3 and `Step-ID: 01-02`.
steps.implementation_notes: D2: the store is a DUMB ORDERED LOG — `append`/`load_journal` deal in `LoadedEntry` and the store NEVER partitions. A notification and a command append identically; the cursor (01-03) does the classification ONCE at construction. D3: redb key `(WorkflowId, u32)` is the storage append-position over ALL entries (storage = ordering/observability); `next_step` is count-all and its behaviour is UNCHANGED — it counts every `LoadedEntry` (commands AND notifications), because the storage append-position must include notifications for the key to stay a dense append index. The command-index (replay identity) is DERIVED at the cursor, NOT here — conflating append-position with cursor-consumption-position IS the trap (D3 rationale). The `probe` sentinel changes only in type (`LoadedEntry::Command(JournalCommand::Terminal{...})`); the write->fsync->readback->delete handshake is unchanged. The trait docstrings are the SSOT for the dumb-store contract per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Trait definitions specify behavior' — pin the append-position-vs-command-index distinction explicitly so a future HA adapter (#205) re-implements the log without re-deriving replay semantics. #205 (HA cross-node resume, VERIFIED OPEN) is node-independent and not precluded by this split — cite by number, create nothing. Keep `JournalStoreError` / `ProbeError` unchanged.
quality_gates: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-sim --lib passes for the store layer
next_step count-all behaviour unchanged (counts commands + notifications)
redb key (WorkflowId, u32) unchanged
trait docstrings pin the dumb-store contract (pre/post/edge/invariant)
single commit
ADR-0066 cited in body
deliverables: JournalStore trait deals in LoadedEntry (docstrings pinned)
RedbJournalStore value type LoadedEntry; next_step unchanged

steps.name: Partition at the JournalCursorHandle: Vec<JournalCommand> walk + BTreeMap<SignalKey,JournalNotification> lookup; retire *cursor += 2
steps.description: D2 (cursor half) + D6 (notification model). Move the partition into `JournalCursorHandle::new` / `new_with_channels` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs`: at construction, partition the loaded flat `Vec<LoadedEntry>` ONCE into a `Vec<JournalCommand>` (the positional replay walk) + a `BTreeMap<SignalKey, JournalNotification>` (the correlated notification lookup — `BTreeMap`, not `HashMap`, per `development.md` § 'Ordered-collection choice'). The cursor index now walks `Vec<JournalCommand>` only and advances by exactly 1 per replayed command. Rewrite the `replay_run` / `record_run` / `replay_sleep` / `record_sleep_armed` / `replay_signal` / `record_signal_awaited` / `record_signal_seen` / `replay_emit` / `emit_action` methods so: (a) command replay hits read from `replay_commands[cursor]` and advance by 1; (b) `replay_signal` resolves `SignalSeen` by `SignalKey` lookup in the `BTreeMap`, NEVER by position — RETIRING the `*cursor += 2` two-positional-entry signal walk; a `SignalAwaited` command present with no matching `SignalSeen` notification re-blocks; (c) `record_*` live paths append the appropriate `LoadedEntry` (commands advance the command-cursor; `record_signal_seen` appends a `Notification` and does NOT advance the command-cursor). `next_step`-derived `step:u32` is gone — `record_*` no longer stamps a `step` field. Extend the `JournalCursor` trait docstrings (`overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs`) with the command-advance contract (cursor advances over `JournalCommand`s ONLY; notifications never advance it) and the notification-lookup contract (resolved by `SignalKey`, never by position; a notification is never consumed as a command). Implements D2 (cursor) + D6 (notification map).
criteria: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs` — `JournalCursorHandle` holds `replay_commands: Vec<JournalCommand>` + `signal_notifications: BTreeMap<SignalKey, JournalNotification>` (NOT a flat `Vec<JournalEntry>`); both `new` and `new_with_channels` partition the passed `Vec<LoadedEntry>` ONCE at construction.

The `*cursor += 2` in `replay_signal` is RETIRED — `replay_signal` resolves `SignalSeen` via `signal_notifications.get(signal_key)` (correlated lookup), and the command-cursor advances by exactly 1 past the `SignalAwaited` command; a `SignalAwaited` command with no matching `SignalSeen` notification returns `None` (re-block).

Every command replay hit (`replay_run`, `replay_sleep`, the `SignalAwaited` arm of the signal path, `replay_emit`) advances the command-cursor by exactly 1; `record_signal_seen` appends a `LoadedEntry::Notification` and does NOT advance the command-cursor.
No `record_*` method stamps a `step: u32` field (the field is gone from the variants per 01-01); the command-index is the partition position.

`crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs` — `JournalCursor` trait docstrings extended with the command-advance contract (post: a replay hit at command-index N returns the recorded `JournalCommand`'s result and advances the command-cursor by exactly 1; invariant: the cursor advances over `JournalCommand`s ONLY) and the notification-lookup contract (post: `SignalSeen` resolved by `SignalKey` lookup, never by position; a `SignalAwaited` with no matching `SignalSeen` re-blocks; invariant: a notification is never consumed as a command).

`crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/workflow_engine_replay_cursor.rs` — a scenario drives a run whose loaded `Vec<LoadedEntry>` interleaves commands and a `SignalSeen` notification and asserts: the command-cursor walks only the commands, the notification is resolved by `SignalKey` (not position), and no `+2` positional advance occurs; GREEN.

`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-core --lib` is GREEN for the cursor layer (engine `start` Started-write updated in 01-05; determinism fail-closed updated in 01-04 — adjacent surfaces may be RED scaffolds until then).
Commit-message shape: `refactor(workflow-cursor): partition LoadedEntry at the cursor (command walk + signal-key lookup); retire +2 (D2/D6)`. Body cites ADR-0064 §3/§4 and `Step-ID: 01-03`.
steps.implementation_notes: D2: the partition lands HERE, ONCE at construction — `JournalCursorHandle::new`/`new_with_channels` consume the flat `Vec<LoadedEntry>` and split it into `Vec<JournalCommand>` (positional walk) + `BTreeMap<SignalKey, JournalNotification>` (correlated lookup). `BTreeMap`, not `HashMap`, per `development.md` § 'Ordered-collection choice' — the map is iterated/observed by DST and must be deterministic across seeds. The retired `*cursor += 2` (currently in `replay_signal`/`replay_run`'s signal handling around `workflow_runtime/mod.rs:697`) was the two-positional-entry signal walk; under the split, `SignalAwaited` is a command (advances by 1) and `SignalSeen` is a notification resolved by `SignalKey` (off the walk). 'Crashed while blocked' = `SignalAwaited` command present, no matching `SignalSeen` notification -> `None` -> re-block (the S-WP-03-01 crash-safety shape, now expressed structurally instead of by `cursor+1`-with-no-following-entry). D6: build ONLY the `BTreeMap<SignalKey, JournalNotification>` — no `NotificationId`, no general Restate-style correlation model (rejected, not deferred). The `JournalCursor` trait docstrings are the contract SSOT per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Trait definitions specify behavior'; pin command-advance and notification-lookup invariants so the host handle and any future sim cursor honor the same behaviour. The Layers 1+2 determinism gate is a SEPARATE concern landed in 01-04 — keep the existing name-mismatch `NonDeterministic` check working here, but the type-at-index Layer 1 is 01-04's deliverable. Do NOT reshape production to suit the sim (`development.md` § 'Production code is not shaped by simulation') — the partition is the production-correct shape, the sim follows.
quality_gates: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-core --lib passes for the cursor layer
*cursor += 2 retired (grep clean in workflow_runtime/mod.rs)
replay_signal resolves by SignalKey lookup, not position
JournalCursor trait docstrings pin command-advance + notification-lookup contracts
BTreeMap (not HashMap) for the notification map
single commit
ADR-0064 cited in body
deliverables: JournalCursorHandle partitions LoadedEntry once at construction

steps.name: Determinism gate Layers 1+2 fail-closed (type-at-index + name-in-RunResult) returning NonDeterministic
steps.description: D4. Add the fail-closed determinism gate to the cursor's command-replay path in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs`. Layer 1 (type-at-index, Restate RT0016 shape): the recorded `JournalCommand` variant at command-index N must match the resumed body's await-op kind (a `ctx.run` await landing on a recorded `SleepArmed`, or a `ctx.sleep` landing on a recorded `RunResult`, etc., is a divergent trajectory); on mismatch the cursor returns `WorkflowCtxError::NonDeterministic { expected, actual }` and does NOT advance and does NOT fall through to the live path — closing the trap's twin (the former silent fall-to-live). Layer 2 (name within `RunResult`): the recorded `name` must match the replaying body's `ctx.run` name; on mismatch, the same `NonDeterministic` fail-closed (this check exists today as the name-mismatch arm — preserve and re-home it under the explicit Layer-2 framing). Layer 3 (content/digest comparison) is DEFERRED to #214 (already created, VERIFIED) — do NOT implement it. Extend the `JournalCursor` trait docstring with the determinism fail-closed contract (post: on a Layer-1 or Layer-2 mismatch the cursor returns `NonDeterministic { expected, actual }`, does NOT advance, does NOT fall through to live; invariant: a divergent journal is an error, never a silent re-execution). Implements D4 (CA-6).
criteria: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs` — every command-replay method checks the recorded variant TYPE at the command-cursor against the await-op being replayed (Layer 1); a type mismatch returns `WorkflowCtxError::NonDeterministic { expected, actual }`, does NOT advance the cursor, and does NOT return `Ok(None)` (no fall-through to the live path).

Layer 2 (name within `RunResult`) preserved: a recorded `RunResult` whose `name` differs from the replaying `ctx.run` name returns `NonDeterministic { expected, actual }` fail-closed (the existing check re-homed under the explicit Layer-2 framing).

Layer 3 (content/digest) is NOT implemented — no `result_digest` / `value_digest` / `action_digest` comparison is added at the cursor; #214 is cited in the code comment marking the boundary (VERIFIED OPEN; create nothing).

`crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs` — `JournalCursor` trait docstring carries the determinism fail-closed contract (post + invariant as above), naming Layers 1+2 in scope and Layer 3 (#214) out.

`crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/workflow_engine_replay_cursor.rs` — a Layer-1 scenario (a recorded `SleepArmed` at the cursor while the body replays a `ctx.run` await) asserts `NonDeterministic { expected, actual }` with no advance and no fall-through; a Layer-2 scenario (recorded `RunResult` name != body name) asserts the same; both GREEN.
`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-core --lib` is GREEN.
Commit-message shape: `feat(workflow-cursor): fail-closed determinism gate Layers 1+2 (type-at-index + name) -> NonDeterministic (D4)`. Body cites ADR-0064 §3 + #214 and `Step-ID: 01-04`.
steps.implementation_notes: D4: the gate is fail-closed — a divergent journal is an ERROR, never a silent re-execution. Layer 1 (type-at-index) is the load-bearing new check: it closes the trap's twin, where a variant mismatch at the positional cursor formerly fell through to `Ok(None)` (the live path) and silently re-executed. Under the typed split this becomes structural — the await-op being replayed names its expected `JournalCommand` kind, and a mismatch against `replay_commands[cursor]` is a hard `NonDeterministic { expected, actual }`. Layer 2 (name within `RunResult`) already exists in `replay_run` (the `recorded_name != name` arm at ~`workflow_runtime/mod.rs:600`); preserve it and re-frame it as Layer 2 in the docstring. `WorkflowCtxError::NonDeterministic { expected, actual }` already exists — reuse it, do not add a variant. Layer 3 (content/digest) is OUT — DEFERRED to #214 (already created, verified open); do NOT add a digest comparison; cite #214 at the code boundary only, create nothing, add no new forward pointer. The `expected`/`actual` payload must be deterministic (a stable variant-kind label or recorded name, never an address-bearing Debug of the whole entry) so DST trajectories stay byte-identical across seeds (`development.md` § label enums own their string representation — prefer an `as_str()`-style kind label). Do NOT advance the cursor on any mismatch.
quality_gates: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -p overdrive-core --lib passes
Layer 1 type-at-index mismatch returns NonDeterministic, no advance, no fall-through
Layer 2 name-in-RunResult mismatch preserved fail-closed
Layer 3 NOT implemented (#214 cited, nothing created)
JournalCursor trait docstring pins the determinism fail-closed contract
single commit
ADR-0064 + #214 cited in body

steps.name: WorkflowEngine::start writes Started at command-index 0 on first start, idempotent on resume
steps.description: CA-4 (the trap itself). Make `WorkflowEngine::start` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs` write a `Started` command (a `LoadedEntry::Command(JournalCommand::Started { spec_digest, input_digest })`) at command-index 0 on FIRST start, and be IDEMPOTENT on resume (a loaded run whose first command is already `Started` is NOT re-written). Today `Started` is documented as the journal's first entry but `start` never writes it — that is the trap. After this step the engine appends `Started` before spawning the author body on first start; on resume the loaded `Vec<JournalCommand>` already begins with `Started`, the cursor walks it as command-index 0, and the first `await`-point reads command-index 1. The `spec_digest` / `input_digest` are the workflow-kind + start-input identities (inputs, per `development.md` § 'Persist inputs, not derived state'). The append goes through the sanctioned `journal.append` path (fsync-before-spawn). The engine test asserts `Started` is present at command-index 0 after first start AND is not duplicated after a resume. Implements CA-4 (D6 engine half).
criteria: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs` — `WorkflowEngine::start`, on FIRST start (loaded run empty), appends `LoadedEntry::Command(JournalCommand::Started { spec_digest, input_digest })` via `journal.append` BEFORE spawning the author body; the append is fsync'd before the spawn (ADR-0066 §4 fsync-then-suspend ordering).

Idempotent on resume: when the loaded run's first command is already `Started`, `start` does NOT append a second `Started` (the check is structural — first element of the partitioned `Vec<JournalCommand>` is `Started`).

After first start, `load_journal` returns a run whose first `LoadedEntry` is `Command(Started{..})` and the cursor treats it as command-index 0; the first author `await`-point records at command-index 1.
`spec_digest` and `input_digest` are derived from the `WorkflowSpec` + start input (inputs), not from any post-hoc/derived state.

`crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/workflow_engine_writes_terminal_row.rs` (or a sibling engine acceptance test) — a scenario asserts `Started` at command-index 0 after first start AND no duplicate `Started` after a resume drives `start` a second time over the persisted journal; GREEN.
`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --lib` is GREEN; `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace` is GREEN (the full reshape now compiles end-to-end).
Commit-message shape: `feat(workflow-engine): write Started at command-index 0 on first start, idempotent on resume (CA-4)`. Body cites ADR-0066 §2 / ADR-0064 §5 and `Step-ID: 01-05`.
steps.implementation_notes: CA-4 closes the trap: `Started` becomes a REAL engine-written command-index-0 entry instead of an aspirational-but-never-written first entry. The append happens in `WorkflowEngine::start` (currently `workflow_runtime/mod.rs:279`), AFTER `load_journal` and BEFORE building the cursor handle + spawning the body — so the cursor partitions a run that already begins with `Started`. Idempotency on resume is structural: inspect the loaded `Vec<JournalCommand>` (or the flat `Vec<LoadedEntry>` before partition) — if its first command is `Started`, skip the append; the SAME instance always re-derives the SAME `WorkflowId` (via `WorkflowId::for_correlation`, unchanged) so resume targets the same run. fsync-before-spawn preserves the durability ordering (ADR-0066 §4): a crash after the `Started` append but before the spawn re-loads a run beginning with `Started` and resumes cleanly. `spec_digest`/`input_digest` are inputs per `development.md` § 'Persist inputs, not derived state' (the existing `Started` variant already carries them). Do NOT introduce a `step` field (gone in 01-01). This step makes `cargo check --workspace` green end-to-end — it is the last call-site update in the Outside-In chain (the enum split's downstream breakage from 01-01 is fully resolved here). Per `feedback_dont_pause_between_steps`, proceed directly to 01-06 after commit.

idempotent on resume (no duplicate Started)

steps.name: Extend replay_equivalence_provision_record DST invariant with the Started-at-0 + notification-not-as-command cursor-advance guard; verify no consumer reads dropped step
steps.description: D6 + D5 verification. EXTEND the existing `replay_equivalence_provision_record` DST invariant (verbatim name — `evaluate_replay_equivalence_provision_record` in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs`, NOT a new invariant family) to drive a run whose journal has `Started` at command-index 0, crash after step-N, resume, and assert: (a) the resumed `ctx.run` effect fires 0 times (K1 — already asserted, keep it); (b) the resumed command sequence is byte-identical INCLUDING `Started` at command-index 0 (K4 — extend the existing recorded-RunResult equality to a full command-sequence equality that pins `Started` at index 0); AND (c) zero re-executions caused by a non-command (a `SignalSeen` notification) consumed as a command — i.e. the notification is resolved by `SignalKey` off the walk and never advances the command-cursor. This is the structural guard that would have caught the original trap (a dropped `Started` write, or a `SignalSeen` entering the positional command walk, fails this invariant). It is on the CI critical path (`cargo dst`). ALSO perform the D5 follow-through verification: confirm no consumer reads the dropped in-entry `step:u32` — the store counts entries for `next_step` (not a per-entry field read) and the cursor derives the command-index from partition position; grep the workspace for any residual per-entry `step` read and confirm zero. Implements D6 (CA-7) + the D5/CA-2 DELIVER verification obligation.
criteria: `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` — `evaluate_replay_equivalence_provision_record` (verbatim name, NOT a new family) is extended: the resumed-vs-uninterrupted equality is widened from 'recorded RunResult matches' to 'the full command sequence matches, with `Started` present at command-index 0 in both', AND a new assertion verifies a `SignalSeen` notification interleaved in the loaded run is resolved by `SignalKey` and never consumed as a command (the command-cursor advance count equals the number of commands, excluding the notification).
Assertion (a): the resumed run re-fires the recorded `ctx.run` effect ZERO times (K1) — preserved.

Assertion (b): the resumed command sequence is byte-identical to the uninterrupted one INCLUDING `Started` at command-index 0 (K4) — the invariant fails if `Started` is missing at index 0 or the command sequence diverges.

Assertion (c): zero re-executions caused by a non-command consumed as a command — a `SignalSeen` notification in the loaded run does NOT advance the command-cursor and is resolved by `SignalKey`; the invariant fails if a notification is walked as a command.

D5 verification: a workspace grep confirms no consumer reads a per-entry `step:u32` (the field is gone; `next_step` is count-all; the command-index is partition-derived) — documented in the step's commit body as 'D5/CA-2 verified: zero per-entry step readers'.

`crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/replay_equivalence_provision_record_invariant.rs` — the acceptance driver for the extended invariant asserts the Started-at-0 + notification-not-as-command guard; GREEN.
`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim --features integration-tests` (the DST lane) is GREEN; the extended invariant is on the `cargo dst` critical path.
`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` is GREEN (full reshape lands green end-to-end).

After this step lands, run the per-feature mutation gate ONCE: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests` returns kill-rate >= 80% (run with `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Mutation testing is the exception').

Commit-message shape: `test(workflow-dst): extend replay_equivalence_provision_record with Started-at-0 + notification-not-as-command guard (D6); verify no step reader (D5)`. Body cites ADR-0064 §6 and `Step-ID: 01-06`.
steps.implementation_notes: D6: EXTEND the SAME named invariant `replay_equivalence_provision_record` — the verbatim-name constraint is load-bearing (it is the structural guard that would have caught the trap; a NEW family would not be on the existing CI critical path and would not be the regression the trap demands). The existing evaluator (`evaluate_replay_equivalence_provision_record`, ~`evaluators.rs:767`) already drives the three-run crash-resume shape (uninterrupted / crash-injected / resumed) and asserts K1 (effect fires 0 on resume) + a recorded-RunResult equality; widen the equality to the FULL command sequence pinning `Started` at command-index 0 (use/extend the `entry_kinds` helper updated in 01-01 to compare command-kind sequences), and ADD the notification-not-as-command assertion (drive a run with an interleaved `SignalSeen`, assert the command-cursor advance count == command count and the signal resolves by `SignalKey`). Seeded + reproducible (`cargo dst --seed <N>`); SINGLE-NODE only (D3 / #205 — cite, create nothing). D5/CA-2 follow-through: this is an in-scope DELIVER CHECK, not a deferral — grep the workspace for any reader of a per-entry `step` field (there should be none: the store counts via `next_step`, the cursor derives the command-index from partition position) and record the zero result in the commit body. The mutation gate is per-feature, diff-scoped, run ONCE here at the end (NOT per step); kill-rate >= 80%; per `feedback_no_git_checkout_after_mutants` do NOT run `git checkout -- crates/` after the run (cargo-mutants restores the tree itself; the destructive-git-ops hook blocks it). Per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`, any clippy issue surfaced by the workspace-wide checks lands in this step, not a follow-up. Per `feedback_no_unrequested_ci_lefthook`, do not wire anything new into CI/lefthook beyond the existing `cargo dst` lane that already runs this invariant.
